videos.title: Finding Pattern Rules | Predict and Extend Patterns
videos.description: Master the skill of finding pattern rules! Learn to look at a pattern, figure out the rule, and predict what comes next in number and shape patterns.
learningObjectives: Find the rule in any type of pattern (number, shape, or mixed)
Use the rule to predict and extend patterns
keyConceptsCovered: Steps to find a rule: observe, compare, test, extend
Rules can involve addition, subtraction, repetition, or transformation

videos.title: How to Describe Pattern Rules in Words | Explaining Patterns
videos.description: Learn to describe pattern rules using clear words. Practice explaining rules like 'add 3 each time' or 'rotate clockwise, then change color' with examples.
learningObjectives: Describe pattern rules in complete sentences
Use mathematical vocabulary to explain patterns
keyConceptsCovered: Checking your rule works for all parts of the pattern

videos.title: Patterns Everywhere | Rules in Music, Dance, and Daily Life
videos.description: See how pattern rules appear in music rhythms, dance moves, daily schedules, and even cooking recipes. Patterns and rules are everywhere once you know how to look!
learningObjectives: Recognize pattern rules in non-mathematical contexts
Describe rules in music, movement, and daily life
keyConceptsCovered: Dance steps follow repeating and growing patterns
Daily routines and schedules have pattern rules
prerequisites: Pattern rules in numbers and shapes

videos.title: Pattern Rule Detective | Find the Rule Challenge
videos.description: Become a pattern detective! Each puzzle gives you a pattern and you must figure out the rule. Covers number patterns, shape patterns, and mixed patterns.
learningObjectives: Apply rule-finding strategies to various pattern types
keyConceptsCovered: Systematic approach: look at differences, look at the core, test the rule
Mixed patterns require checking multiple attributes

videos.title: Create a Pattern from a Rule | Reverse Pattern Challenge
videos.description: The ultimate pattern challenge! Given a rule, can you create the pattern? Try rules like 'start at 5, add 4 each time' or 'alternate between two shapes, rotating each one.'
learningObjectives: Verify that created patterns follow the given rule
keyConceptsCovered: Working backwards from rule to pattern
Starting from a given number and applying the rule
Creating shape patterns from transformation rules

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track with real-life applications, practice, and reverse challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of finding pattern rules with practice and challenge

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core pattern rule concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: The pattern is: 7, 11, 15, 19, 23. What is the rule?
questions.explanation: Look at the difference between each pair: 11-7=4, 15-11=4, 19-15=4, 23-19=4. The rule is 'add 4 each time'.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: This is the capstone topic for the chapter. Encourage students to describe rules in their own words before using mathematical terms. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students. The reverse challenge (creating patterns from rules) is excellent for deeper understanding.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Play 'Pattern Detective' at home: one person creates a pattern and the other finds the rule. Then swap! Try with numbers, shapes, sounds, and even actions.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: To find a rule, ask yourself: What is changing? How much is it changing by? Is it the same change every time? Once you know the rule, you can extend any pattern!
